event: Peripheral blood smear (PBS) revealed 97% L1-type blasts
Pre-B-cell ALL was detected by immunophenotyping
transaminases were within the normal range
blood urine nitrogen was within the normal range
creatinine was within the normal range
uric acid was within the normal range
phosphor was within the normal range
no cells were seen in the cerebrospinal fluid
benign cytology was reported by pathology
A modified BFM protocol, Turk ALL 2000, was given to the patient
PBS smear on the 8th day revealed poor steroid response
no hematologic remission occurred in bone marrow aspiration at the 15th day
no hematologic remission occurred in bone marrow aspiration at the 33rd day
patient was included in the high-risk group of the treatment protocol
BCR-ABL fusion gene was found to be positive
cytogenetic analysis of the first-day bone marrow aspiration revealed t(9;22) in 11 out of 20 metaphases
the first course of High Risk (HR1) block therapy was given following induction therapy
imatinib (Glivec® 400 mg/m2/day per os) was started
required several units of erythrocyte and platelet transfusion
C-reactive protein (CRP) was found to be positive (26 mg/dl, N: <0.5 mg/dl)
the first HR2 block treatment could not be started
remission was seen in bone marrow aspiration which was done 37 days after the first HR1 block had been completed
deep tendon reflex were found in all extremities
Cranial magnetic resonance imaging was normal
severe mucositis, hemorrhagic diarrhea, typhlitis and hyperemesis were still ongoing
arterial blood pressure was 70/30 mm Hg
blood gas analysis revealed metabolic acidosis (lactic acid: 12.6 mmol/l)
she was transferred to the Pediatric Intensive Care Unit
Intensive fluid replacement, positive inotropic support, erythrocyte and thrombocyte suspensions were given
Left ventricular ejection fraction was found to be 64% by echocardiography
the encephalopathy progressed and generalized tonic-clonic convulsions occurred
she was intubated and mechanically ventilated
Cranial computed tomography showed a 9-mm bleeding region and peripheral edema in the left basal ganglion
lactic acidosis progressed and lactic acid levels reached up to 20 mmol/l
thiamine deficiency secondary to long-term TPN without vitamin B complex was considered
4 × 25 mg/day intravenous thiamine treatment was started
lactic acidosis regressed rapidly and normalized
thiamine therapy was given regularly for 3 months
lactic acidosis never developed in the follow-up
persistent severe mucositis and related gastrointestinal hemorrhage
the bone marrow was in remission
the BCR-ABL gene fusion was negative
the patient was followed up under mechanic ventilation for 1 month
staying for 54 days in the intensive care unit
cognitive functions returned back to normal after 6 months
mucositis showed slow regression over months
physiotherapy was regularly performed for spastic tetraparesis
patient was sent home while she was still on imatinib therapy
able to walk by herself without any support
started to feed orally and the PEG was closed
bone marrow examination was performed every third month
morphological and molecular remission was observed at each evaluation
no matched donor could be found
still in morphological and molecular remission
